title: Brief
description: A voice-optimized research brief generated twice a week by the Clockchain Research Brief agent. Renders into the /brief route of clockchain-research.

slug.description: Filename stem. Format: YYYY-MM-DD-<short-slug>. Must equal the JSON filename without .json.

title.description: Voice-readable headline. Under 90 chars preferred. Often ends with an em-dash phrase.

dek.description: One-sentence subhead framing the argument. UI-rendered; numerals are OK here.

date.description: ISO date. Used as the primary sort key (newest first) on the index page.

dayOfWeek.description: Display only. Typically 'Tuesday' or 'Friday' for scheduled briefs.

topic.description: One of the rotation tracks from config/topics.yaml. Three buckets: customer-profile-*, use-case-*, standards-*. Legacy track keys retained so older briefs remain valid.

tldr.description: 150-250 words of spoken-style prose. Short sentences. No lists. Spell out numbers as words ('thirty percent' not '30%').

briefAlso.description: When true, render as a brief-only card (no full article) on the index page.

sections.description: 4-6 sections is the target. Each has a short heading and a body of 4-8 sentences of prose. NO bullets, tables, or numbered lists in body copy. The LAST section MUST be 'What this means for our agent products'.

heading.description: Short noun phrase or natural spoken cue. Example: 'What this means for our agent products'.

body.description: Prose paragraphs only. NO bullets, tables, or numbered lists. Numbers spelled out as words.

keyPoints.description: 3-5 short declarative sentences for quick recall. UI-rendered; numerals OK here.

nextUp.description: Exactly 3 follow-up questions, each phrased exactly as the user would speak it aloud to a voice agent.

sources.description: 6-10 real URLs with descriptive titles. Real sources only.
